text: Planning a trip is always exciting, and my last adventure to Japan was no exception. I spent weeks researching destinations and booking accommodation, eager to experience a new culture. The food was incredible—from steaming bowls of ramen to delicate sushi—but navigating menus required pointing and smiling due to language barriers. Despite my limited Japanese, locals were patient and helpful. Those memories, like wandering through ancient temples and tasting street food at night markets, remain vivid. Travel taught me that even simple conversations can bridge differences, leaving lasting impressions.
